subject: Biology
title: Ocean Currents: Rivers in the Sea
description: Learn Ocean Currents: Rivers in the Sea in one minute.

hook.text: The Gulf Stream moves more water than all the world's rivers combined—and it heats all of Europe.

buildup.text: Surface currents are driven by wind and Earth's rotation. Deep currents are driven by temperature and salinity.

discovery.text: The thermohaline circulation is a global conveyor belt that takes 1,000 years to complete one loop.

twist.text: If the conveyor belt slows—as melting ice may cause—Europe could cool dramatically despite global warming.

climax.text: Currents distribute heat, nutrients, and oxygen—they are the circulatory system of the planet.

punchline.text: Invisible rivers running through the sea, shaping the climate.
